Items.Title: PostgreSQL 备忘单 | PostgreSQL Cheat Sheet
Items.Description:
在使用 PostgreSQL 数据库时，我们收集了一系列方便的 PostgreSQL 命令和查询。 如果您想在备忘单中添加一些内容，请联系我们@ForestAdmin ！

登录并输入 PostgreSQL 命令行实用程序 psql

 $ sudo su - postgres psql

列出 postgres 数据库

连接到 postgres 数据库

退出 postgreSQL 命令行实用程序 psql

管理 PostgreSQL

检查 PostgreSQL 版本

检查是否安装了 PostgreSQL

检查 PostgreSQL 是否正在运行

$ pgrep -fa -- -D | grep postgres

在 Linux 上重新启动 PostgreSQL

 # systemctl restart postgresql // or # service postgresql restart // or # /etc/init.d/postgresql restart // or # /sbin/service postgresql restart

在 OSX 上重新启动 PostgreSQL

 # brew services restart postgres // or # pg_ctl -D /usr/local/var/postgres -l /usr/local/var/postgres/server.log restart

在 Windows 上重新启动 PostgreSQL

 Winkey + R Type "services.msc" Click "restart"

更改 PostgreSQL 用户密码

$ sudo -u postgres psql \password postgres

退出 psql

与 PostgreSQL 中的数据库交互

列出 postgres 数据库

连接或切换到 postgres 数据库

创建新的 postgres 数据库

CREATE DATABASE databaseName

删除 postgres 数据库

DROP DATABASE databaseName

重命名 postgres 数据库

ALTER DATABASE old_databaseName TO new_databaseName

查询 postgres JSON 数据

SELECT * FROM mytable WHERE myfield @> '{"key1":"valueA"}'

与 PostgreSQL 中的表交互

列出 postgres 表

描述 postgres 表

创建 postgres 表

CREATE TABLE tableName( columnName columnType, columnName columnType );

删除 postgres 表

DROP TABLE tableName CASCADE

备份和恢复 PostgreSQL 数据库

备份 postgres 数据库

$ pg_dump -d databaseName -U userName > backupFile

恢复 postgres 数据库

psql databaseName < backupFile

在 PostgreSQL 中管理角色和权限

列出 postgres 角色

SELECT rolname FROM pg_roles;

创建 postgres 用户

CREATE USER userName WITH PASSWORD 'userPassword';

删除 postgres 用户

更改 postgres 用户密码

ALTER ROLE userName WITH PASSWORD 'userPassword';

列出所有分配的角色 postgres

 SELECT r.rolname, r.rolsuper, r.rolinherit, r.rolcreaterole, r.rolcreatedb, r.rolcanlogin, r.rolconnlimit, r.rolvaliduntil, ARRAY(SELECT b.rolname FROM pg_catalog.pg_auth_members m JOIN pg_catalog.pg_roles b ON (m.roleid = b.oid) WHERE m.member = r.oid) as memberof, r.rolreplication FROM pg_catalog.pg_roles r ORDER BY 1;

授予对 postgres 数据库的所有权限

GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON DATABASE databaseName TO userName;

授予对 postgres 数据库的连接权限

GRANT CONNECT ON DATABASE databaseName TO userName;

列出特定角色 postgres 的权限

SELECT table_catalog, table_schema, table_name, privilege_type FROM information_schema.table_privileges WHERE grantee = userName ORDER BY table_name;

列出所有可用的 psql 命令

需要为 PostgreSQL 构建管理面板或 GUI 工具？ 查看PostgreSQ L 的 Forest Admin



原文:

We gathered a list of handy PostgreSQL commands and queries when working with PostgreSQL databases. Please ping us @ForestAdmin if you’d like to add something to the cheat sheet!

Getting started with PostgreSQL

Login and enter PostgreSQL command line utility psql

$ sudo su - postgres psql

List postgres databases

Connect to postgres database

Exit postgreSQL command line utility psql

Managing PostgreSQL

Check PostgreSQL version

Check if PostgreSQL is installed

Check if PostgreSQL is running

$ pgrep -fa -- -D | grep postgres

Restart PostgreSQL on Linux

# systemctl restart postgresql
// or
# service postgresql restart
// or
# /etc/init.d/postgresql restart
// or
# /sbin/service postgresql restart

Restart PostgreSQL on OSX

# brew services restart postgres
// or
# pg_ctl -D /usr/local/var/postgres -l /usr/local/var/postgres/server.log restart

Restart PostgreSQL on Windows

Winkey + R
Type "services.msc"
Click "restart"

Change PostgreSQL user password

$ sudo -u postgres psql
\password postgres

Exit from psql

Interacting with databases in PostgreSQL

List postgres databases

Connect or switch to postgres database

Create new postgres database

CREATE DATABASE databaseName

Delete postgres database

DROP DATABASE databaseName

Rename postgres database

ALTER DATABASE old_databaseName TO new_databaseName

Query postgres JSON data

SELECT *
FROM mytable
WHERE myfield @> '{"key1":"valueA"}'

Interacting with tables in PostgreSQL

List postgres tables

Describe postgres table

Create postgres table

CREATE TABLE tableName( 
    columnName columnType,
    columnName columnType
);

Delete postgres table

DROP TABLE tableName CASCADE

Backup and restore PostgreSQL database

Backup postgres database

$ pg_dump -d databaseName -U userName > backupFile

Restore postgres database

psql databaseName < backupFile

Managing roles and permissions in PostgreSQL

List postgres roles

SELECT rolname FROM pg_roles;

Create postgres user

CREATE USER userName WITH PASSWORD 'userPassword';

Delete postgres user

Change postgres user password

ALTER ROLE userName WITH PASSWORD 'userPassword';

List all assigned roles postgres

SELECT
    r.rolname,
    r.rolsuper,
    r.rolinherit,
    r.rolcreaterole,
    r.rolcreatedb,
    r.rolcanlogin,
    r.rolconnlimit,
    r.rolvaliduntil,
    ARRAY(SELECT b.rolname
        FROM pg_catalog.pg_auth_members m
        JOIN pg_catalog.pg_roles b ON (m.roleid = b.oid)
        WHERE m.member = r.oid) as memberof,
    r.rolreplication
FROM pg_catalog.pg_roles r
ORDER BY 1;

Grant all permissions on postgres database

GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON DATABASE databaseName TO userName;

Grant connection permissions on postgres database

GRANT CONNECT ON DATABASE databaseName TO userName;

List permissions for specific role postgres

SELECT table_catalog, table_schema, table_name, privilege_type
FROM   information_schema.table_privileges
WHERE  grantee = userName ORDER BY table_name;

ProstgreSQL psql commands recap

\? List all available psql commands

\h COMMAND Get help on specific command

\l List databases

\c databaseName Connect to database

\dt List tables

\d tableName Describe table

\d+ tableName Describe table with details

\dn List schemas

\df List functions

\dv List views

\du List users

\dy List events

\di List indexes

\q Exit

金枪鱼是海洋生态系统的重要组成部分，也是最具经济价值的物种之一。 鲣鱼、黄鳍金枪鱼、大眼金枪鱼、太平洋金枪鱼、大西洋金枪鱼和南方蓝鳍金枪鱼都是商业金枪鱼渔业中的重要物种。 公司增加了对研发 (R&D) 工作的投资，以应对对健康饮食和可持续捕捞作业日益增长的需求。 消费者的需求促使实施复杂的金枪鱼加工工艺以应对不断变化的饮食偏好。 近年来，金枪鱼罐头的全球消费量呈爆炸式增长。 罐装金枪鱼是一种流行的低成本蛋白质，易于储存，不需要冷藏。 它是一种方便出口的产品，因为它几乎不需要冷藏，这有助于促进发达国家和发展中国家对罐装金枪鱼产品的需求。 劳动人口的大幅增加推动了对加工、即食、即食商品的需求。

Items.Title: 使用 Vue.js 进行 Jest 测试 | Jest testing with Vue.js
Items.Description:
我对 Jest 测试完全陌生，但我目前正在开发一个 Vue 项目。
我需要测试的组件同时使用了 vuex 和 Vue mixins。

我已经构建了测试文件，导入了我需要的文件并模拟了 vuex，但由于混合，我收到了失败。

错误是“无法读取未定义的属性（读取'状态'）

我的 mixin 中有与 vuex 对话的函数，所有这些都是因为它在项目中的多个地方使用。

我遇到的问题是它在 mount 上调用了 mixin，因此测试尝试使用该 mixin/vuex 而不是我的模拟 vuex。

有没有人有这方面的经验可以帮忙吗？

我已经在谷歌上搜索了关于如何模拟 mixin 环境的 TON，但是关于如何模拟 vuex 没有真正的答案，所以我想我会转向这里看看是否有人可以提供帮助🙂



原文:

I’m completely new to Jest tests but I’m currently working on one with a Vue project.
The component I need to test uses both vuex and Vue mixins.

I’ve built the test file, imported in what I need and mocked up the vuex but I’m receiving a fail due to the mixin.

The error is “can’t read properties of undefined (reading ‘state’)

I have functions in my mixin that speak with the vuex, all of this is because it’s used in multiple places within the project.

The issue I have is it calls the mixin on mounted and so the test is trying to use that mixin/vuex and not my mocked vuex.

Does anyone have experience with this that could help please?

I’ve googled a TON on how to mock up the mixin environment but there’s no real answer on this just how to mock vuex so I thought I’d turn to here to see if anyone can help 🙂
Items.Author: louiseann93

Items.Title: 将 GitLab 与 Bytebase 集成到类似于代码的版本控制架构更改 | Integrating GitLab with Bytebase to Version Control Schema Change Similar to Code
Items.Description:
我们在上一篇文章中介绍了Bytebase中的 UI 工作流：
基于Database-as-Code的概念，Bytebase 为您提供了另一个高级版本控制工作流（GitOps）。

本文将指导您完成 GitLab 和 Bytebase 之间的版本控制工作流程。 确保您安装了具有管理员角色的工作 GitLab 和具有所有者角色的工作 Bytebase。

我们仍将使用我们的示例数据库 Employee
在 GitLab 中创建一个名为 EmployeeBytebase 的项目。

它现在几乎是空的； 我们将一路添加更多文件。

字节库

要启动工作流程，我们首先需要进行一些配置。

工作区级别

步骤 1 添加 Git 提供程序

  • 在 Settings->Version Control 下添加 Git 提供程序，您需要拥有所有者角色才能执行此操作。

  • 遵循三个步骤。 您需要有 GitLab 管理员角色才能在那里进行配置并完成整个过程。

项目级别

创建一个名为 EmployeeGitLab 的项目。

  • 在我们 配置的实例 Test 和 Prod中准备两个数据库，和我们之前做的类似，但是我们可以调整数据库名称来区分。

  • 在这两个数据库中传输。

  • 在这个新项目中，在版本控制下，选择 GitOps 工作流。

  • 选择我们刚刚创建的 GitLab 项目。

    • 确保分支正确。 默认的基本目录是 bytebase； 我们会保留它。

    我们知道按照说明需要两种类型的模式文件。

    • 我们的第一个脚本应该是基线迁移脚本并遵循这种格式。

    我们为当前的两个数据库做一个 mysqldump 并重命名它们。 对于本文，我们遵循文档中推荐的结构。

  • 这是我们的。

  • 我们承诺并推动。

  • 然后我们回到我们的项目主页，啊！ 在最近的活动中，出现了问题。 是的，我们的文件名与模板不匹配。

    • 让我们通过再添加一个“_”、提交并再次推送来解决这个问题。

    • 如果我们在 GitLab 中查看文件，我们会看到 Bytebase 自动创建了一个新的 LATEST SQL。

    • 它在文档中进行了解释。

    • 在我们更改文件名并将它们推送到 GitLab 之后。 单击问题/107 将我们带到问题详细信息页面 - 测试环境。

    • 单击 issue/106 将我们带到 Prod 环境； 状态为等待审批。 这是因为我们将 Prod 环境设置为需要手动批准。

    • 因为我们是项目所有者，所以我们有批准的权限。

    • 如果我们转到项目迁移历史选项卡，我们可以查看所有迁移历史。

    步骤 5 创建第一个正常迁移

    • 设置基线后，现在是真正的架构更改。 我们的第一个任务是为员工添加昵称列。 它与第 4 步类似，不同之处在于文件名应使用迁移而不是基线。 让我们先试试Test。

    • 在 Prod 上重复该过程并手动批准。



    原文:

    We have introduced UI workflow in Bytebase in the previous article:
    How to use Bytebase to facilitate SQL review process (UI workflow)

    Based on the concept of Database-as-Code, Bytebase provides you with another advanced Version Control workflow (GitOps).

    This article will guide you through the version control workflow between GitLab and Bytebase. Ensure you have a working GitLab installed with an admin role and a working Bytebase with an owner role.

    We'll still use our sample database Employee in
    Import sample database into AWS RDS MySQL using Shell & Workbench

    GitLab

    • Create a project called EmployeeBytebase in GitLab.

    It's now almost empty; we'll add more files along the way.

    Bytebase

    To start the workflow, we need first do some configuration.

    Workspace level

    Step 1 Add Git Provider

    • Add a Git provider under Settings->Version Control, you need to have owner role to do this.

    • Follow the three steps. You need to have GitLab Admin role to do configuration there and complete the whole process.

    Project level

    Step 2 Enable Version Control Workflow in Project

    • Create a project called EmployeeGitLab.

    • Prepare two databases in our configured instances Test and Prod, it's similar to what we did before, but we can adjust the database name to distinguish.

    • Transfer in these two databases.

    • Within this new project, under version control, choose GitOps workflow.

    • Choose the GitLab project we just created.

    • Make sure the branch is correct. The default base directory is bytebase; we'll keep it.

    Step 3 Name and Organize Schema Files

    We know that two types of schema files are needed following the instructions.

    • Our first script should be a baseline migration script and follow this format.

    Step 4 Create the First Baseline Migration

    • We do a mysqldump for our current two databases and rename them. For this article, we follow the recommended structure in our documentation.

    • This is ours.

    • We commit and push.

    • Then we come back to our project home page, ah! In Recent activities, there is something wrong. Yes, our file names don't match the template.

    • Let's fix this by adding one more '_', commit, and push again.

    • If we view the files in GitLab, we see Bytebase created a new LATEST SQL automatically.

    • It's explained in the documentation.

    • After we change the file names and push them to GitLab. Bytebase finally receives the trigger and creates issues automatically.

    • Clicking issue/107 brings us to the issue detail page - Test environment.

    • Clicking issue/106 brings us to the Prod environment; the status is Waiting Approval. This is because we set the Prod environment to require manual approval.

    • Because we're the project owner, we have the permission to approve.

    • If we go to the project Migration History tab, we can view all the migration history.

    Step 5 Create the First Normal Migration

    • After setting the baseline, now it's the real schema change. Our first task is to add nickname column to employee. It's similar to Step 4, except the file names should use migrate instead of baseline. Let's first try on Test.

    • Repeat the process on Prod and approve manually.

Items.Title: 在 CI/CD 中运行过多安全工具的挑战 | The Challenge of running too many security tools in CI/CD
Items.Description:
由于需要进行多种类型的 AppSec 扫描来查找所有类型的漏洞，组织运行大量安全工具的情况并不少见。 这意味着来自不同供应商的商业工具、各种开源工具和许多本土脚本和检查的混合。
这种“工具蔓延”分散了 DevSecOps 自动化并阻止了它。 随着安全工具数量的增长和 DevSecOps 流程变得更加复杂，公司意识到更大的挑战是在短时间内理解所有测试结果，例如在管道运行期间。

在围绕自动化安全工具的过程中自动化手动任务对于 CI/CD 中的 DevSecOps 非常重要。 来自 DevSecOps 流程和技术的数据的质量和有效性将成为使用它的人与你的持续改进之间的区别，或者团队只是绕过它而这一切都是浪费时间。

让我们了解手动软件测试中的挑战，并检查自动化此 DevSecOps 流程时的问题。

  • 选择在 CI/CD 中运行的工具
  • 在 CI/CD 内外运行各种工具
  • 将所有安全工具的测试结果收集到一个地方
  • 找出此软件版本中的更改
  • 对所有安全问题进行分类
  • 安全问题的优先级
  • 与不同团队的沟通
  • 安全指标

选择在 CI/CD 中运行的工具

我们已经知道您可能需要至少 10 多个安全集成工具才能为单个项目获得足够的安全覆盖。 很有可能，您的公司将有不同的团队运行不同的技术堆栈。 您可能有为他们设计的不同 CI/CD 管道。 试图以“一刀切”的方式硬装一套安全工具对任何人都不起作用。 例如，针对 C# 代码行运行 python 安全工具将浪费时间并提供错误的安全感。

不同类型的工具更善于发现不同类型的漏洞。 公司正在结合 DAST、SAST、SCA、IAST、容器、云以及更多类型的工具来查找问题。

这就是为什么您需要引入更多工具来满足不同团队所需的安全检查范围。 您的 DevSecOps 技术人员不仅需要处理这些工具，还需要轻松配置用于团队或项目的工具。 然而，尝试在 CI/CD 管道逻辑中配置这些是复杂而僵化的。

这很容易配置，无需修改 CI/CD 逻辑，只需在 CI/CD 工作流程中添加两行，即可为您提供更大的灵活性和对要完成的测试的控制。

在 CI/CD 内外运行各种工具

安全工具有各种形状和大小，您可能需要为每个工具提供逻辑才能让它们运行。 那么你把这个逻辑放在哪里呢？ 许多团队最初尝试将其放在 CI/CD 中，但随着它的复杂性增加，它往往更适合 CI/CD 平台之外，因此可以轻松更新和扩展。

这就是我们在 Kubernetes 和容器之上构建 Uleska 平台架构的原因。 用户可以在 CI/CD 构建期间实时受益于运行安全测试的大小调整、排队和监控，而无需亲自动手。

将所有安全工具的测试结果收集到一个地方

如果您要在管道中使用三个 SAST 工具，它们中的每一个都可能会以不同的方式报告问题。 由于没有关于如何报告安全问题的行业标准，他们可能会执行以下操作：

  • 报告不同的字段
  • 以不同方式报告严重性
  • 几乎没有报告太多

从这三个工具中，需要检索一个综合列表。 必须就如何解决手头的问题以及需要哪些工具进行呼吁。 这是灵活性至关重要的地方，因为您需要添加更多工具。

本质上，这意味着实施 DevSecOps 漏洞管理。 现在，您的工具扩展到动态基础架构、容器和其他类型。 它们可能没有代码字段，但现在我们正在处理端口号、易受攻击的 HTTP 请求等等。

对于一些公司来说，将所有这些工具的结果汇总在一起的任务是一项全职工作，但却是自动化的理想选择。 这就是 Uleska 平台这样做的原因，它使用单一分类法来描述所有结果都映射到的安全问题。

对于这些信息的消费者（包括开发和安全团队）来说，在 CI/CD 管道期间拥有一个统一且一致的玻璃面板来近乎实时地标记安全问题是非常宝贵的。

了解此软件版本中的更改内容

工程和运营团队没有时间在每次 CI/CD 运行中管理大量安全工具中的数百个问题。 软件安全的 DevSecOps 专注于“发生了什么变化？” 问题。 上次更改的合并问题集不需要是项目中每个问题的战争与和平。 请记住，您正在构建一个有效的流程来标记安全问题。

安全工具旨在发现他们可以发现的每一个问题并进行报告。 是的，我们希望在项目开始时对所有问题都有一个基线，但是一旦我们进行了第一次分类，删除了误报、重复和非问题，然后我们希望将这些已知问题移到我们的积压工作中并专注于任何事情新的。

这意味着当安全工具应用于此运行时，我们想知道两件事：

  • 是否出现任何新的安全问题？

新问题可能来自引入新错误的代码或配置更改，或者安全工具（或数据库）已更新并在我们现有的容器和库中发现了新缺陷。

  • 是否已解决任何问题？

这听起来很简单，但如果安全工具之前发现了问题，那么有人已经应用了修复程序。 现在该工具没有发现该问题，因此我们可以考虑确保修复并将其从我们的列表中删除。

使用合适的软件（例如 Uleska 平台），历史安全工具包运行被存储并可与当前运行进行比较，因此您可以立即知道是否引入了新问题。

对所有安全问题进行分类

您如何以及在哪里记录误报、重复和无意义的问题？ 一些商业工具允许您这样做，而许多工具则不允许。 绝大多数开源和自定义安全工具也无法跟踪这一点。

然而，非问题是 DevSecOps 中最大的问题。 开发团队不喜欢从安全运行中获得 100 个非问题。

市场领先的平台允许您为所有安全工具及其运行的结果集中设置误报和非问题。 这些是粘性的，这意味着下次您运行这些工具时，所有非问题都会被记住，并且不会出现在任何报告中，从而在通信中屏蔽这些问题的开发。

在 Uleska，我们正在研究多种新方法来提高误报处理的效率，因此安全和开发团队不需要在这方面浪费时间。

您的安全问题的优先级

如果您曾经尝试针对同一个目标运行许多工具，您会发现一些有趣的事情：

  • 并非所有工具都会发现所有相同的问题（您可以使用它来确定工具的有效性）。
  • 确实发现相同问题的多个工具可能会以不同的方式对其进行优先排序。

这种优先级差异不仅令人沮丧，而且通常仅基于技术错误，并且完全忽略了项目的风险方面。 它忽略了用户数量、处理数据的敏感性、项目对业务的重要性等。

也许低风险问题可以在没有干预的情况下通过，但最高 10% 的风险应该在发布前回到开发阶段进行修复。 像 Uleska 平台这样的软件在 CI/CD 管道和进一步的指标中自动提供了这一点 - 加快了决策制定过程。

与不同团队的沟通

我们已经研究了如何减少每次 CI/CD 运行中被标记的问题的数量，但是，被标记的实际问题的数量仍然会增加 - DevSecOps 以越来越快的速度和规模运行。

请求帮助描述问题并传达最佳实践或建议的修复已经到位可能会消耗大量时间，使安全团队远离其他重要任务并减慢开发速度。

这使团队可以快速访问他们需要的建议、修复和教育，并允许安全团队在不浪费时间的情况下扩展他们的通信。

安全指标

即使从指标的角度来看，贵公司的利益相关者也不希望必须使用许多不同的安全工具来制作统计数据。 它效率低下，又成为某人的工作，这阻止了他们做更有成效的事情。 将所有信息汇总在一起可能需要几天时间，这意味着它已经过时了。

在 CI/CD 管道期间自动执行此操作不仅可以提供近乎实时的安全指标，而且由于它在每个版本中都会更新，因此您对测量有更多的粒度。 每天查看第一个月和第三个月之间的差异比每季度测量一次要好得多。

要了解有关自动化 DevSecOps 的挑战以及如何克服这些挑战的更多信息，请查看我们的手册。

详细了解 DevSCOPS 面临的挑战

值得庆幸的是，您可以采取多种措施来克服面临的挑战。 在我们的剧本中，我们涵盖了自动化 DevSecOps 的 10 大挑战，同时还提供了有关如何克服这些挑战的可行建议。

Download it here! 在这里下载！



原文:

DevSecOps involves setting up many different automated security tools to cover all bases. It’s not uncommon for organisations to run tons of security tools due to the many types of AppSec scans that are needed to find all the types of vulnerabilities. This means a mix of commercial tools from different vendors, various open-source tools and many homegrown scripts and checks.
This ‘Tool Sprawl’ fragments the DevSecOps automation and holds it back. As the number of security tools grows and the DevSecOps processes become more complex, companies are realising the greater challenge is to make sense of all of the test results within a short period of time, like during the pipeline run.

Automating manual tasks in the process around automated security tools is very important for DevSecOps in CI/CD. The quality and effectiveness of the data from your DevSecOps processes and tech are going to be the difference between people using it and your continuous improvement, or teams simply bypassing it and it all being a waste of time.

Let’s go through the challenges in manual software testing and examine the issues when automating this DevSecOps process.

  • Choosing which tools to run in CI/CD
  • Running the various tooling in or out of CI/CD
  • Collect the test results from all the security tools into one place
  • Working out what’s changed in this software release
  • Triaging all your security issues
  • Prioritisation of your security issues
  • Communication with various teams
  • Security Metrics

CHOOSING WHICH TOOLS TO RUN IN CI/CD

We already know you’ll likely need at least 10+ security integrated tools to get enough security coverage for a single project. Chances are, your company will have different teams running different tech stacks. You may have different CI/CD pipelines designed for them. Trying to shoehorn a set of security tools as ‘one size fits all’ will not work for anyone. For example, running python security tools against a C# codeline will be a waste of time and offer a false sense of security.

Different types of tools are better at finding different types of vulnerabilities. Companies are combining DAST, SAST, SCA, IAST, Container, Cloud, and many more types of tools to find issues.

That’s why you’ll need to introduce lots more tooling to cater for the range of security checks varying teams will need. Your DevSecOps tech will not only need to handle those tools but also easily configure which are being used for a team or project. However, trying to configure these within the CI/CD pipeline logic is complicated and rigid.

The Uleska Platform allows you to pre-configure security ‘Toolkits’ that combine relevant security tools according to your projects’ tech stacks and dependencies. This is easily configurable without modifying the CI/CD logic and gives you more flexibility and control over the testing to be done, by simply adding two lines into your CI/CD workflows.

RUNNING THE VARIOUS TOOLING IN OR OUT OF CI/CD

Security tools come in all shapes and sizes and you’ll likely need logic for each and every one to get them to run. So where do you put this logic? Many teams initially try to place it in CI/CD, but as it grows in complexity, it tends to fit better outside the CI/CD platform so it can be updated and extended easily.

Kubernetes and dockers tend to suit the needed architecture of running these security tools well, but creating that setup will become a project in itself. This is why we have built the Uleska Platform architecture on top of Kubernetes and containers. Users can benefit from the sizing, queuing and monitoring of running security tests during the CI/CD builds in real-time - without having to get their hands dirty.

COLLECT TEST RESULTS FROM ALL THE SECURITY TOOLS INTO ONE PLACE

If you were to use three SAST tools in your pipeline, it’s likely that each of them is going to report issues in different ways. As there’s no industry standard on how to report security issues, they may do the following:

  • Report different fields
  • Report severity in different ways
  • Barely report much at all

From those three tools, one consolidated list needs to be retrieved. A call must be made on how to fix the issues at hand and what tools are required. This is where flexibility is vital, as you’ll need to add in more tools.

Essentially, this means implementing DevSecOps vulnerability management. Now your tooling extends to dynamic infrastructure, containers and other types. There’s likely no code fields with them, but now we’re handling port numbers, vulnerable HTTP requests and more.

For some companies, the task of bringing together the results from all of these tools is a full-time job, but one ideal for automation. That’s why the Uleska Platform does exactly that, with a single taxonomy for describing security issues that all results are mapped into.

For consumers of this information, including development and security teams, having a single and consistent pane of glass to flag security issues in near real-time during the CI/CD pipeline is invaluable.

WORKING OUT WHAT’S CHANGED IN THIS SOFTWARE RELEASE

Engineering and operations teams don’t have time to manage hundreds of issues across lots of security tools in every CI/CD run. DevSecOps for software security focuses on the ‘what changed?’ question. Consolidated sets of issues from the last change don’t need to be a War and Peace of every problem in the project. Remember, you’re building an efficient process to flag security issues.

Security tools are built to find every issue they can and report back. Yes, we want a baseline of all issues at the start of the project, but once we do that first triage, remove the false positives, duplicates and non-issues, we then want to move those known issues to our backlog and focus on anything new.

That means there are two things we want to know when security tools are applied to this run:

  • Have any NEW security issues come up?

New issues can come from code or config changes introducing new bugs or a security tool (or database) has been updated and found a new flaw in our existing containers and libraries.

  • Have any issues been fixed?

It sounds simple, but if a security tool has found an issue before, someone has applied a fix. Now the tool doesn’t find that issue so we can consider the fix assured and remove it from our lists.

With the right software, such as the Uleska Platform, historical security toolkit runs are stored and can be compared against the current run, so you can know instantly if new issues have been introduced.

TRIAGING ALL THE SECURITY ISSUES

How and where do you record false positives, duplicates and nonsense issues? Some commercial tools allow you to do this, while many don’t. The vast majority of open source and custom security tools can’t track this either.

Yet non-issues are the biggest problem in DevSecOps. Development teams don’t appreciate being given 100s of non-issues from security runs.

Market-leading platforms allow you to set false-positives and non-issues centrally, for all security tools and results it runs. These are sticky, meaning the next time you run the tools, all the non-issues are remembered and don’t show up in any reports, shielding development from these in communications.

Here at Uleska, we’re working on a number of new ways to make false-positive handling much more efficient, so security and development teams don’t need to waste time on this.

PRIORITISATION OF YOUR SECURITY ISSUES

If you’ve ever tried running many tools against the same target, you’ll find a few interesting things:

  • Not all tools will find all the same issues (you can use this to determine the effectiveness of the tools).
  • Multiple tools that do find the same issue will likely prioritise it differently.

This difference in prioritisation is not only frustrating, but it’s typically only based on the technical bug and completely ignores risk aspects of the project. It neglects the number of users, the sensitivity of data processed, the criticalness of the project to the business and so on.

Maybe low-risk issues can be passed through without intervention, but the highest top 10% of risks should go back to development for fixes before release. Software like the Uleska Platform provides this automatically within the CI/CD pipeline and further metrics - speeding up the decision making process.

COMMUNICATION WITH VARIOUS TEAMS

We’ve already examined how you can reduce the number of issues being flagged in each CI/CD run, however, there will still be an increase in the number of real issues that are flagged - DevSecOps runs at increasing speed and scale.

Requests to help describe the issue and communicating best practices or suggested fixes already in place can consume a lot of time, taking security teams away from other important tasks and slowing down development.

The Uleska Platform has built-in advisory functions that allow categories of custom remediations to be automatically incorporated into communications to development teams. This gives teams quick access to the advice, fixes, and education they need and allows security teams to scale their communication without consuming their time.

SECURITY METRICS

Even from a metrics point of view, the stakeholders in your company don’t want to have to go to lots of different security tools and make up the statistics. It’s inefficient and again becomes someone’s job, which prevents them from doing something more productive. It can take days to bring all the info together, meaning it’s already out of date.

Automating this during the CI/CD pipeline not only gives near real-time metrics on security, but since it’s being updated every release, you have a lot more granularity on the measurements. Seeing the difference between month one and month three, daily, is a lot better than measuring the difference once a quarter.

To discover more about the challenges of automating DevSecOps and how to overcome them, check out our playbook.

LEARN MORE ABOUT THE CHALLENGES OF DEVSECOPS

The problem with DevSecOps is incorporating many layers of security tasks into the fast-paced software development cycle. Thankfully, there are a variety of things you can do to overcome the challenges faced. In our playbook, we cover the top 10 challenges of automating DevSecOps, while also delivering actionable advice on how to overcome them.

Download it here!

Items.Title: 大型重构期间的技术领导 | Technical leadership during large refactors
Items.Description:
很久以前，我们开始了一个项目，将我们的 API 序列化器从ActiveModelSerializers移动到Blueprinter 。 这种事情很难。 很难在高流量端点上迁移一堆现有的序列化程序，并确信没有什么会在你的脸上爆炸。 很难让人们有动力迁移单个文件并完成工作。 很难确定您是否正确地传达了更改，并且在您迁移时没有人会引入更多的技术债务。

这些都是我们在迁移时必须处理的问题。 在我们开始使用新库的情况下，我们的迁移很长一段时间都处于一种不稳定的状态。 然而，大量的遗留序列化程序集尘埃落定，并没有像它们在关键或常用路径中那样被迁移。 人们普遍担心进入那里并改变一切。

我与已经为迁移做出贡献的团队成员聊天，反思了我自己的经历，并确定了一些共同的问题。 在本文中，我将介绍我们如何解决完成项目时遇到的主要障碍：

保持势头并不容易，因为不清楚最终目标是什么。 即使有现有的测试，人们也不相信他们的更改会投入生产并且一切都会好起来的。

目前尚不清楚从哪里开始或如何参与。

设定目标职位并建立动力

因此，在尝试开始解决实际迁移任何文件的问题之前，我想处理两个直接的问题。

  • 我们怎么知道我们什么时候完成了工作？
  • 我们如何知道人们在迁移时不会对旧库增加更多的技术债务？

这两个都是需要解决的重要问题。 当人们不知道还剩多长时间，不知道最终状态是什么样子时，很难激励他们完成一项工作，或者在这种情况下，我们如何知道何时可以安全地移除旧库？ 我们希望尽可能多地消除其他开发人员在此期间必须编写序列化程序的摩擦。 没有人愿意在开始做某事时对使用哪一项技术感到困惑，他们也不希望在他们刚刚完成工作后在代码审查中向他们指出这一点。

我想起了我最近在另一个项目中所做的一些工作，在该项目中我使用自定义 Rubocop 类来解决类似问题。 它们很方便，因为您可以在代码库中持续执行某个方向，防止任何人做您试图避免的事情，同时还在他们将收到的错误消息中提供更多上下文。 最重要的是，Rubocop 将为现有文件生成的违规列表将反映我们需要完成的工作主体，然后我们才能认为项目已完成。

这是我们最终得到的结果：

 # frozen_string_literal: true require 'rubocop' module RuboCop module Cop module Serializers class PreferBlueprinter < Base BASE_PATTERN = '(const (const nil? :ActiveModel) :Serializer)' MSG = 'Use Blueprinter, please!' def_node_matcher :class_definition , <<~ PATTERN (class (const _ _) #{ BASE_PATTERN } ...) PATTERN def on_class ( node ) class_definition ( node ) do add_offense ( node . children [ 1 ]) end end end end end end

我还在习惯写这些。 尽管如此，Evil Martians 的这篇文章还是帮了大忙。 我们组合的 cop 检查该类是否继承自ActiveModel::Serializer并在该行中添加攻击。

现在要获取我们需要迁移的类的待办事项列表。 运行bundle exec rubocop --format files --only Serializers/PreferBlueprinter将生成对我们的新警察造成攻击的文件列表。 我们可以将它添加到我们的 rubocop.yml 文件中，作为该警察的排除列表。

 Serializers/PreferBlueprinter : Exclude : - ' app/serializers/old_serializer_a.rb' - ' app/serializers/old_serializer_b.rb' - ' app/serializers/old_serializer_c.rb'

该列表现在是我们将如何衡量我们离完成的距离。 对迁移的每个贡献都会减少该文件，直到我们在忽略列表中没有任何内容。 之后，我们可以进行最终 PR，从我们的代码库中删除 cop 和旧库。

提高信心

对于每个期望的更改，使更改变得容易（警告：这可能很难），然后进行简单的更改。
肯特贝克

好的，但是我们仍然没有解决人们对更改这些承载序列化程序的信心不足和恐惧的根本问题。 通过与团队的交谈，我学到了两件事：

  • 人们不太关心完全破坏事物。 有足够的测试来发现问题，虽然很烦人，但我们会收到关于这类事情的警报并且可以回滚。
  • 他们更关心更微妙的序列化问题。 我们如何确定我们不会在新的序列化程序中引入一些可能导致问题的小差异？

所以，我们能做些什么？ 我认为我们可以添加某种形式的安全网，以便如果我们的更改确实出现问题，我们会 a) 知道以便我们可以修复它们并且 b) 不会对生产产生不利影响。 我登陆了一个关于暗读的版本，除了这里，我们正在比较两个不同序列化程序的结果，而不是从两个数据库中读取。 如果我们检测到任何差异，我们会丢弃新的并回退到旧的 JSON。

下面的帮助类是我们最终得到的通用版本（对一些决定有额外的评论）：

 class BlueprinterMigrationFallback class BlueprinterValidationMismatch < StandardError def initialize ( message , diff ) @diff = diff . map do | operator , field , _ | " #{ operator } #{ field } " end super ( message ) end attr_reader :diff end # For the original serialisation, we can just pass in the JSON output as is, # but, I chose to use a proc for the new serialisation so that we don't go through # the work of serialising it for requests that we don't need to. def self . validate ( ams_json :, blueprinter_proc :, ignore: []) sample_rate = Rails . env . production? ? 0.1 : 1.0 sampled = Random . rand < sample_rate return ams_json unless sampled blueprinter_json = blueprinter_proc . call # We found that some serialised fields _could_ be non-deterministic for # whatever reason, so we added support to ignore fields from a diff as # they would be different with each serialisation. diff = Hashdiff . diff ( ams_json , blueprinter_json ). reject { | _ , field , _ | ignore . include? ( field ) } # Raise our custom exception if there's a diff between the two versions. raise BlueprinterValidationMismatch . new ( 'Resource failed to serialise with Blueprinter' , diff ) unless diff . empty? # Everything's all good, use our new serialisation! blueprinter_json # Rescuing from StandardError here in case the proc we pass in throws some # error we're not expecting. rescue StandardError => exception # Log the exception + the keys and operators involved in the diff. In our # app, this is where we capture the exception in Sentry so we can follow up later. raise exception if Rails . env . development? || Rails . env . test? # Return the original JSON. ams_json end end

这是一个非常简单的类，但除非它与旧序列化程序匹配，否则不使用新序列化程序的安全网应该会提高对进行更改的信心。 权衡是您为请求的子集执行了两次工作，这可能是一个潜在的问题，具体取决于您要序列化的内容以及它的繁重程度。

另一个潜在的问题是差异本身，这可能是一个可能需要长时间运行的步骤，具体取决于有效负载的大小。 在一种情况下，我们被这种情况所困扰，索引操作上的每条记录也序列化了数千条相关记录。

让人们在同一页面上

将助手类添加到代码库并自己进行了几次迁移后，我有一些工作可以作为示例供其他人效仿。

我将该信息编译成一份文档，其中概述了迁移其中一个旧类所采取的步骤、为什么我们以这种方式解决问题以及其他人可能遇到的一些其他用例。 该文件还重申了问题陈述，为什么我们首先要更改序列化程序以及我们希望从中得到什么。 该项目陷入困境的时间已经足够长，值得传达可能已经丢失到 Slack 深处的信息。

为什么我们还要这样做？
如果您阅读 了我上一篇关于 Oj gem 执行的 JSON 模块重定义的文章，如果您使用它的.optimize_rails方法，我将详细介绍可能引入的意外行为，特别是在您不打算使用它的地方发生（即泄漏到另一个宝石中）。

这个项目是出于其他原因开始的，但是，这种框架有助于赋予它新的生命。

我们是怎么走的？

这些变化都不意味着工作可以在一夜之间立即完成，但它们为完成工作提供了一个框架。 该项目再次进行，我们已经完成了需要迁移的文件的三分之一（一组 150 个文件中的 50 个）。

到目前为止，我对结果如何感到非常满意，并且该过程为我提供了更多工具来管理大型跨团队变更。 当我们到达终点线时，我一定会报告我学到的任何新知识！

总结一下，这些是我们将其视为更好地管理大型跨团队变更的方法的一些关键点：

  • 确保您可以衡量和报告进度。 您需要一个最终目标，并且报告您的移动方式可以帮助保持势头。
  • 将风险降至最低。 让人们更容易失败，如果他们担心的事情更少，他们会更倾向于提供帮助和参与。
  • 记录更改。 确保你能够清楚地说明你在做什么，为什么，以及有什么好处。

让我知道您是否有自己的经历或挑战，以一种让人们参与进来或让其他人更容易参与的方式进行变革管理。

Paweł Świątkowski - Paweł Świątkowski - Feb 15 2月15日



原文:

A Long Time Ago™, we started a project at work to move our API serialisers from ActiveModelSerializers to Blueprinter. This kind of thing is hard. Hard to migrate a bunch of existing serialisers in prod on high traffic endpoints and be confident nothing will blow up in your face. Hard to get people motivated to migrate individual files and get things done. Hard to be sure that you've communicated the change correctly and that no one is left introducing more technical debt while you migrate.

These are all issues that we had to deal with doing our migration. Our migration languished for a long time in a kind of limbo state where we had started using the new library. However, an extensive collection of legacy serialisers sat gathering dust, not migrated as they were in critical or commonly-used paths. There was a general trepidation around getting in there and changing things over.

I chatted to team members who had contributed to the migration already, reflected on my own experiences, and identified some common concerns. In this article, I'll go through how we address the main hurdles we had with getting the project completed:

It wasn't easy to maintain momentum because it wasn't clear what the end goal was. Even with the existing tests, people weren't confident that their changes would go out to production and everything would be fine.

It was unclear where to start or how to get involved.

Setting goal posts and building momentum

So there were two immediate issues I wanted to deal with before trying to start approaching the problem of actually migrating any files over.

  • How do we know when we've finished the work?
  • How do we know people aren't adding more technical debt with the old library while we're migrating?

Both of these were important problems to solve. It's hard to motivate folks to finish a job when they don't know how long is left to go, what the end state looks like, or in this case, how do we know when we're safe to remove the old library? And we want to remove as much friction as we can for other developers having to write serialisers during this period. No one wants to be confused about which bit of tech to use when starting something, and they don't want to have it pointed out to them in a code review after they've just finished doing their work.

I was reminded of some work I'd done recently in another project where I used a custom Rubocop class to solve similar issues. They're handy because you can enforce a certain direction in the codebase on an ongoing basis, preventing anyone from doing the thing you're trying to avoid, while also providing more context in the error messages they'll receive. On top of that, the list of offences Rubocop would generate for the existing files will reflect the body of work we need to get through before we can consider the project completed.

Here is what we ended up with:

# frozen_string_literal: true require 'rubocop' module RuboCop module Cop module Serializers class PreferBlueprinter < Base BASE_PATTERN = '(const (const nil? :ActiveModel) :Serializer)' MSG = 'Use Blueprinter, please!' def_node_matcher :class_definition, <<~PATTERN (class (const _ _) #{BASE_PATTERN} ...)
 PATTERN def on_class(node) class_definition(node) do add_offense(node.children[1]) end end end end end
end

I'm still getting used to writing these. Still, this article from Evil Martians has been a big help. The rubocop-rails codebase also had some cops similar to what I wanted to put together. The cop we've put together checks if the class inherits from ActiveModel::Serializer and adds an offence to that line.

Now to get our todo list of classes we need to migrate. Running bundle exec rubocop --format files --only Serializers/PreferBlueprinter will generate a list of files that cause offences for our new cop. We can add that to our rubocop.yml file as an exclude list for that cop.

Serializers/PreferBlueprinter: Exclude: - 'app/serializers/old_serializer_a.rb' - 'app/serializers/old_serializer_b.rb' - 'app/serializers/old_serializer_c.rb'

That list is now how we're going to measure how close we are to being done. Each contribution to the migration will whittle that file down until we don't have anything left on the ignore list. After that, we can do a final PR to remove the cop and old library from our codebase.

Raising Confidence

For each desired change, make the change easy (warning: this may be hard), then make the easy change.
Kent Beck

Okay, but we still haven't dealt with the root issue of people's low confidence and fear around changing these load-bearing serialisers. From chatting with the team, I learned two things:

  • People were less concerned about completely breaking things. There were enough tests to catch issues, and while annoying, we'll get alerts about that kind of thing and can roll back.
  • They were more concerned about subtler serialisation issues. How can we be sure that we're not introducing some small difference into the new serialiser that might cause issues?

So what can we do? I thought we could add some form of safety net so that if things did go awry with our changes, we'd a) know so we could fix them and b) not adversely affect production. I landed on a version on dark reading, except here, we're comparing the results of two different serialisers rather than reading from two databases. If we detect any differences, we'd throw away the new one and fall back to the old JSON.

The below helper class is a generalised version of what we ended up with (with extra comments around some of the decisions):

class BlueprinterMigrationFallback class BlueprinterValidationMismatch < StandardError def initialize(message, diff) @diff = diff.map do |operator, field, _| "#{operator} #{field}" end super(message) end attr_reader :diff end # For the original serialisation, we can just pass in the JSON output as is, # but, I chose to use a proc for the new serialisation so that we don't go through # the work of serialising it for requests that we don't need to. def self.validate(ams_json:, blueprinter_proc:, ignore: []) sample_rate = Rails.env.production? ? 0.1 : 1.0 sampled = Random.rand < sample_rate return ams_json unless sampled blueprinter_json = blueprinter_proc.call # We found that some serialised fields _could_ be non-deterministic for # whatever reason, so we added support to ignore fields from a diff as # they would be different with each serialisation. diff = Hashdiff.diff(ams_json, blueprinter_json).reject { |_, field, _| ignore.include?(field) } # Raise our custom exception if there's a diff between the two versions. raise BlueprinterValidationMismatch.new('Resource failed to serialise with Blueprinter', diff) unless diff.empty? # Everything's all good, use our new serialisation! blueprinter_json # Rescuing from StandardError here in case the proc we pass in throws some # error we're not expecting. rescue StandardError => exception # Log the exception + the keys and operators involved in the diff. In our # app, this is where we capture the exception in Sentry so we can follow up later. raise exception if Rails.env.development? || Rails.env.test? # Return the original JSON. ams_json end
end

It's a pretty simple class, but the safety net of not using the new serialiser unless it matches the old one should raise confidence in making the changes. The tradeoff is that you're doing the work twice for a subset of requests, and that's a potential gotcha depending on what you're serialising and how heavy it is.

The other potential gotcha is the diffing itself, which can be a potentially long-running step depending on how big the payload is. We got bitten by that in one case where each record on the index action was also serialising thousands of associated records.

Getting people on the same page

Having added the helper class to the codebase and gone through a couple of migrations myself, I had a body of work I could point to as examples for others to follow.

I compiled that info into a document that outlined the steps one would take to migrate one of the old classes, why we approached the problem this way and some additional use cases that other people might run into. The document also reiterated the problem statement, why we were changing serialisers in the first place and what we were hoping to get out of it. The project had been in limbo for long enough that it was worth communicating that info that might have been lost to the depths of Slack.

Why are we even doing this?
If you've read my last post about the JSON module redefinition the Oj gem performs if you use it's .optimize_rails method, I went into some detail around the potentially unexpected behaviour that that introduces, particularly in places you're not intending for it to happen (i.e. leaking out into another gem).

This project started out for other reasons, but, this framing helped to give it new life.

How did we go?

None of these changes meant that the work was instantly completed overnight, but they provided a framework for getting the job done. The project is moving along again, and we've made our way through a third of the files we need to migrate (50 of a set of 150).

I'm really pleased with how this has turned out so far, and the process has given me more tools to approach managing a large cross-team change. I'll be sure to report back with anything new I learn as we make our way to the finish line!

Just to wrap up, these were some of the key points we looked at as ways to better manage a large cross-team change:

  • Ensure you can measure and report on progress. You need an end goal in sight and reporting on how you're moving can help keep the momentum up.
  • Minimise risk. Make it easy for people to fail, they'll be more inclined to help out and get involved if they have fewer things to worry about.
  • Document the change. Make sure you're able to articulate what you're doing and why, and what the benefits are.

Let me know if you've had your own experiences or challenges going through change management in a way that gets people on board or makes it easy for others to get involved.

Paweł Świątkowski - Feb 15
Items.Author: antonivanopoulos

Items.Title: 无脚本测试自动化正在改变软件测试 | Scriptless Test Automation is transforming Software Testing
Items.Description:
测试自动化在软件开发领域产生了巨大的影响，因为它可以准确、快速地测试大量数据。 由于自动化测试的实施，测试效率水平提高了一个档次。 随着无脚本测试自动化的加入，事情变得更加容易，并且与测试自动化相关的复杂性也得到了简化。 顾名思义，非技术用户也可以理解无脚本测试自动化，只要向他们提供有关测试自动化活动的基本概念。 在不涉及代码的情况下，它提供了一个平台来鼓励更多的技术人员学习和使用它来以更有效的方式执行软件测试过程。 在本文中，您将了解无脚本测试自动化在软件测试领域转型中的价值。

什么是无脚本测试自动化 (STA)？

这是一种特定方法，允许业务用户和测试人员自动化测试用例，而无需关注编码部分。 获得更快的结果并减少了理解代码所花费的时间。 非测试用户可以通过 STA 的平台了解测试人员在做什么，分析测试用例，也可以清楚地了解测试过程。
随着 STA 的战略实施，整个软件开发过程将扩大规模，代码可重用范围也将增加。 当采用无脚本框架时，开发团队可以转移精力，将更多时间投入到与脚本开发相关的工作上。 该框架还简化了与测试自动化相关的复杂性。

无脚本测试自动化的战略重要性：

关键方面之一是业务用户可以参与其中。 它还充当技术专家和分析师之间交流的促进者，以便他们可以轻松地审查测试用例。 无脚本测试将帮助测试团队更快、更有效地自动化，并提高上市时间和交付速度。

当自动化时间减少时，团队可以在软件开发过程中更早地检测到错误。 由于脚本所涉及的技术复杂性在无脚本框架中并不重要，因此与维护相关的成本也降低了。

利用无脚本测试自动化的需要：

测试人员可以反过来利用他们的技能和时间，为各种测试场景创建自动化测试用例，这反过来将有助于增强产品的核心功能。 产品的开发速度更快，客户将能够收到高质量的产品。

提高了团队的生产力，降低了成本，并适当地满足了不断增长的测试需求。 测试人员无需学习新技能。 组织可以专注于培训他们的手动测试人员以开发某些特定的技能。 但是，如果组织有一个或多个涉及自动化活动的大型项目，那么招聘测试自动化工程师可以被认为是明智之举。 这一切都取决于可用的预算、资源和基础设施、项目范围和管理层的决定。

以下是一些用于无脚本测试自动化的重要工具：

它有一个人工智能 (AI) 系统，通过它可以自动克服应用程序的变化，并帮助团队减少维护时间和降低成本。

它支持 JavaScript 和 VB 脚本。 它可以轻松测试任何 API 和 UI 界面。

结论：如果您期待为您的特定项目实施无脚本测试自动化服务，那么请与专业知名的软件测试服务公司联系，该公司将为您提供符合您项目特定要求的可行测试策略。

关于作者：我是一名技术内容作家，专注于撰写技术特定的文章。 我努力提供有关领先市场的精通技术的经过充分研究的信息。



原文:

Test automation has made a tremendous impact in the software development domain because of its efficacy to test huge amounts of data accurately and quickly. The testing efficiency levels have gone a notch higher because of the implementation of automation testing. With the inclusion of scriptless test automation, things have been easier and upon that the complexities related to test automation are simplified. As the name says, scriptless test automation can be understood by non-technical users also, provided a basic idea is provided to them about the test automation activities. With no code involved, it provides a platform to encourage more technical members to learn and use it for carrying out the software testing process in a more efficient manner. In this article, you will get to know the value of scriptless test automation in transforming the software testing domain.

What is Scriptless Test Automation (STA)?

It is a specific method wherein business users and testers are allowed to automate test cases, without focusing on the coding part. Faster results are achieved and the time spent to understand the code is reduced. Non-testing users can use the platform of STA to understand what the tester is doing, analyze test cases and also understand the testing process clearly.
With the strategic implementation of STA, the entire software development process will scale up and the scope of code reusability is also increased. When script-less frameworks are adopted, the development teams can shift their efforts and put in more time on script development related work. Complexities related to test automation are also simplified by this framework.

The strategic importance of Scriptless test automation:

One of the key aspects is that business users can get involved. It also acts as a facilitator for communication between technical experts and analysts, so that test cases can be easily reviewed by them. Script-free testing will help the testing team to automate more quickly and efficiently as well as improve the go-to-market time and speed of delivery.

When the time to automate is reduced, bugs can be detected earlier in the software development process by the teams. The costs related to maintenance are also reduced as the technical complexity involved in the script does not matter in a scriptless framework.

The need to leverage Scriptless test automation:

The time spent on developing automated tests is reduced by the tactical implementation of STA tools. Testers can in turn use their skills and time so that automated test cases are created for various test scenarios, which in turn will help in enhancing the product’s core features. The product can be developed at a faster pace and customers will be able to receive a high-quality product.

The productivity of teams is increased, the cost is decreased and the increasing testing demands are properly met. There is no need for testers to acquire new skills. Organizations can focus on training their manual testers on developing certain specific skillsets. But, again if the organization has a sizeable project or projects involved in automation activities, then recruiting test automation engineers can be considered to be a wise move. It all depends upon the budget, resources and infrastructure available, project scope and management’s decision.

Following are a few important tools used for scriptless test automation:

1. AccelQ:It is a cloud-based continuous testing platform. API and web testing can be automated without even writing a single line of code. AccelQ can automate many key aspects such as test generation, test design, planning and execution.

2. Ranorex Studio: It provides a set of scriptless test automation tools for performing end-to-end testing on desktops, mobile and websites.

3. Test Craft: It is a scriptless selenium test automation platform through which web applications can be monitored. It has an Artificial Intelligence (AI) system, through which changes in the application can be overcome automatically and helps the team to reduce maintenance time and reduce costs.

4. Zaptest:With this tool, the test automation is optimized through auto-documentation, less scripting and parallel execution. It supports JavaScript and VB Script. It can easily test any API and UI interface.

Conclusion: If you are looking forward to implementing scriptless test automation services for your specific project, then do get connected with a professionally acclaimed software testing services company that will provide you with a viable testing strategy that is in line with your project specific requirements.

About the author: I am a technical content writer focused on writing technology specific articles. I strive to provide well-researched information on the leading market savvy technologies.

Items.Title: 用 Appwrite 收听“我想购买 COVID-19 检测试剂盒”网站！ | Listen the "I want to buy the COVID-19 test kit" website with Appwrite!
Items.Description:
一个月前，Omincron疫情爆发，台湾本地病例每天都在增加。

为了检测 Omincron，COVID-19 检测试剂盒越来越重要。 但是，台湾的试剂盒对人们来说还不够便宜。 为解决上述问题，政府决定征收检测试剂盒。

它可以让人们可以买到便宜的东西。 这些被征用的试剂盒将让一些药店出售，每个药店每天可以从政府那里获得78个试剂盒。

身份证号最后一位为奇数的人，可以在周一、周三、周五和周日到药房购买最多5个试剂盒。 而身份证号码最后一位的人，甚至可以在周二、周四、周六和周日去药房购买最多5个试剂盒。

为了让大家知道哪里有药房可以买到试剂盒，我取了台湾疾控中心的药房试剂盒公开数据。 并开发简单的网站，让人们输入关键字来搜索药房和试剂盒编号。

我的提交概述

网站很简单，我用jQuery开发前端。

然后使用药房检测试剂盒数据作为源数据，让jQuery解析CSV数据，最后呈现在网页中。

我提供网站供人们浏览，可用链接如下：

  • 提交类别：

    它属于 Web2 Wizards 类别。

    链接到代码


    其他资源/信息

    以下是有关开发上述网站的一些参考资料：

    • 台湾国民健康保险开放数据


原文:

Since one month ago, the Omincron epidemic is outbreak and the local cases are increased every day in Taiwan.

To detect the Omincron, the COVID-19 test kit is more and more important. However, the test kit in Taiwan is not cheap enough for people. To solve the above problem, the government decides to expropriate the test kit.

And it can let them be cheap for people to be available to buy. These expropriated test kits will let some pharmacies sell and each pharmacy can get 78 test kits from government every day.

People who have the last number of their ID number is odd can go to the pharmacy and buy the 5 test kits at most on Monday, Wednesday, Friday and Sunday. And the people who have the last number of their ID number is even can go to the pharmacy and buy the 5 test kits at most on Tuesday, Thursday, Saturday and Sunday.

To let people realize where pharmacy is available to buy the test kit, I fetch the open data about pharmacy test kits that is generated by Taiwan CDC. And develop the simple website to let people type keywords to search the pharmacy and test kit numbers.

Overview of My Submission

The website is simple, and I use the jQuery to develop the front-end.

Then using the pharmacy test kit data to be the source data and let the jQuery parse the CSV data then present them in the web page finally.

I provide the website for people to browse and The available links are as follows:

  • The Appwrite dashboard
  • GitHub page

Submission Category:

It belongs to the Web2 Wizards category.

Link to Code


Additional Resources / Info

  • jQuery
  • Taiwan NHI Open Data
